Alright, welcome back. So the unfair advantage that our gyms have over everyone else in the marketplace. So part of the reason that our gyms win more is because they actually have a better profit model. Okay, and so what I'm going to show you right now is kind of a numbers breakdown. So don't believe me, just here's math of how our gyms outspend and outcompete people for the acquisition and then ultimately make more money as a result. And so in this overview, you'll notice that the second part of this is you're going to be signing up with prestige labs so that you can be a licensed distributor. And the reason for that is because, well, there's three reasons. The first is that when you do this, it will allow you to liquidate your ad spend on the front end with what we call the no sale sale. And so what that does is especially for those of you who are new, who haven't you know, honed your sales sales as much or you haven't done cold traffic sales as much, what this does is it gives you more wiggle room. Okay, so what it is essentially is when someone walks in your door, you're obviously going to try and sell them service, right? That's the first thing you're going to try and sell them. If they say no, then what this does is it provides you a second opportunity to make a sale. And that's what we call the no sale sale. And so what we do is we close them on coming back for the nutrition orientation in a separate orientation only for people who said no. And in that orientation, we provide them all the meal plan, grocery list, fruit, prep and structures, everything you were going to provide these people on the front end, which by the way, does make it some way that they're getting this challenge for free just FYI, that's not the only way there's three ways thing in it for free, but that's one of them. And they can do it at home and do the workouts at home. But what you're going to do there is you're actually going to sell them product. Okay, so a lot of times the average ticket for those tends to be higher than the average ticket for people who actually just bought service, which is crazy. Okay, so what that means to me is that these people are just generally say no because they don't want to put the work in. And so they're willing to buy product and work out at home because they just don't want to feel like making a gym. And that's fine. The reason that's important though, is that it will increase the amount of money that you're making per person who walks in the door because that's what matters from a marketing standpoint. So it allows you to take the 50, the 40, the 30% of people who walk out the door or who would normally walk out the door and actually still sell them things to make money and recoup your ad spend. All right, so that's the first reason. If you don't have that, you're at a disadvantage from people who do. All right, the second reason is that it will allow you to add on an extra one or $200 per customer on the front end in receivables to you, not in total sales, receivables to you, which if done as laid out, should likely pay for your entire campaign, right, for your ad spend. And so what that means is the no sale sale is one sale. The second sale that you're going to have is for people who obviously purchased the services. So they signed up for the challenges. They signed up for the program that you're selling, right? Then during the nutrition orientation works the exact same way as the no sales, you're going to run through the nutrition and everything, and then you're just going to sell them the product that would naturally go with the program. All right, so now that first person has now, you've gotten, you've extracted, you've delivered value and extracted value in exchange twice first for service and then for product, okay, twice. For the no sale people, they didn't take service, so that was a no there, but then they say yes for the second sale. So it allows you two opportunities to make money, all right? So this is the second reason why you should do this and why it is an infinite fair advantage for you. The third is it allows you to create a second recurring revenue stream, okay? And so you're going to be able to create a recurring revenue stream from people who said no and from people who said yes, so that 30 days later you get hit a pop of another set of sales that come in as a result of these people being on subscription, all right? Do not underestimate how unbelievable powerful this is and I'm going to show you what this looks like from a money in dollar standpoint, all right? So gym number one, running this play without the unfair advantage without the enhancement of using prestige labs and being real with you, like this is just numbers, like just numbers, all right? So let's say they spent $3,000, they get 200 leads, they schedule 140 of them, they get 70 to show and they sell 35 at $600, so they do 21,000 in service revenue. That's great, don't get me wrong, it's awesome, nothing wrong with that, okay? Now, gym number two, which is enhanced, right, with the unfair advantage, they spend the same money, they spend $3,000, 200 leads, they do the same exact thing except now they have a whole second set of sales, right? They have their challenge prestige sales, which for some reason the box is in display, right? The prestige, you see 27 at $200, which is $5,400 in revenue, all right, that comes in and that's for challenge sales. So that's for people who said yes to service and yes to product, all right? Then after that, you have your no-sales sales, you had 70 people who took that offer and then of the people who came back for the nutrition orientation, let's say you had, you know, half show, you're going to close most of them, believe me, we've done this, you'll close most of them at $200, which is another $6,000 in revenue. Then you're going to close 80% of all of these sales, both the people who bought service and supplements and the people who didn't buy service and bought supplements, 80% is what you should shoot for and that's what a lot of the gyms do into subscription. And so 30 days later, you're now going to have half of those people, I'm just pulling a very conservative number for you, still on subscription and still coming in at $200 a pop, all right? So the product revenue then ties in on top at $17,400. Now, what's interesting about product revenue versus service revenue is that service businesses, especially brick and mortar, tend to be on the average lower total margin as businesses, okay? So most gyms, when they come to us, and this is self-reported, started a 10% margin, all right? So they're making 10% off their gym when they come to us. And so product revenue exists right at a 40%, a little bit higher for some products, but 40% on average, kind of across the board for product revenue. So that's $17,000, you're keeping 40% of that, which is four times 1.7, whatever that is, $6,800. So you're making almost seven grand in extra, right? Assuming you have no extra added costs for doing this, that's take home, okay? So you can see here that not only does that, would that revenue cover the ad spend, it would actually make you profit on the ad spend from that alone, all right? So if you're not implementing this, you're putting yourself at a disadvantage. That is why we're having you set this up so that you can ultimately profit more on the front end from these new customers. We do have plays so that you can actually get your entire membership base on subscription, and that's an entire internal marketing play, which you can do at the very end. Once you've done your gym launch, I do not want to distract you with those things. I need you to execute this first. And so all you're doing here is you're just bolting on sales right on the front end and getting a lot of them on subscription, getting people who are going to say no to say no to service to say yes to products, still provide more value, get tons of goodwill because these people are like, oh my gosh, you're just servicing me free, that's awesome. And you're going to show them how that works, okay? So this is the unfair advantage.
